title: Wire the FAB and create sheet into the switcher
action: Wire create-sheet.ts (previous cell) into switcher.ts: add a FAB button, bottom-right, single entry point (parent D1: docs/history/new-shell-new-agent/CONTEXT.md), using the existing icon-btn visual language, that opens the create sheet as an in-view overlay (same show/hide pattern as terminal.ts's reply-sheet -- not a router navigation to open). Per S4, the FAB must be disabled (not hidden) whenever herdr is unreachable. switcher.ts's existing loadHealth() only toggles CSS classes on #health-dot today (fire-and-forget, exposes no reusable value) -- extend it to also drive the FAB's disabled state from the same fetchHealth() result, rather than adding a second health check. Add an onCreated prop to SwitcherProps typed as (ref: NewPaneRef) => void (import NewPaneRef from web/src/main.ts, same type cell 3's create-sheet.ts already produces -- pass it straight through, do not re-derive or repackage it). Wire it in web/src/main.ts's switcher case to call navigate() with cell 2's widened Route ({name: 'terminal', agent: ref} or whichever variant cell 2 chose), landing the Operator in the new pane's terminal detail on a successful create (parent D6).

truths: The FAB is visible and enabled whenever herdr is reachable; disabled (not hidden) whenever health-down, driven by the same loadHealth()/fetchHealth() call already made on load
Tapping the FAB opens the create sheet as an in-view overlay without re-fetching or altering the switcher list beneath it
A successful create closes the sheet and navigates to the new pane's terminal detail using cell 3's NewPaneRef unmodified

artifacts.substantive: renders the FAB and the create sheet, health-gated per S4

key_links: switcher.ts imports and renders create-sheet.ts's component
main.ts's switcher case passes an onCreated callback that calls navigate with the widened Route, carrying the NewPaneRef through unchanged
prohibitions: Do not change existing switcher behavior: agent list, grouping, collapse, refresh, logout, pull-to-refresh
Do not modify create-sheet.ts's or api.ts's internals beyond importing/wiring them
Do not redefine or restate NewPaneRef's field set -- import cell 2's type

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/switcher.test.ts

trace.outcome: Wired the FAB and create sheet into switcher.ts: added a bottom-right FAB (D1) using the icon-btn visual language, health-gated (disabled, not hidden, when herdr is unreachable) by extending the existing loadHealth()/fetchHealth() call rather than adding a second check (S4). The FAB opens create-sheet.ts's renderCreateSheet() as an in-view overlay (same show/hide pattern as terminal.ts's reply-sheet), and SwitcherProps gained an onCreated: (ref: NewPaneRef) => void prop passed straight through to the sheet. main.ts's switcher case wires onCreated to navigate({ name: 'terminal', agent: ref }), landing on the new pane's terminal detail on a successful create (D6). Auto-added web/src/styles.css (deviation, see deviations file) for FAB positioning/disabled state and the sheet's overlay chrome, since neither this cell nor cell 3 had it in their files list despite needing it.

deviations.description: Auto-added web/src/styles.css, which was absent from this cell's (and cell 3's) files list despite being required for the FAB's bottom-right fixed position and disabled visual state (a must-have truth) and for the create sheet to render as an in-view overlay per the cell action's explicit instruction to mirror terminal.ts's reply-sheet. Every prior UI-adding cell in this repo's history (e.g. terminal-reply-ui-1, terminal-nav-keys-2) included styles.css alongside its view file; its omission here and in cell 3 looks like a planning gap, not an intentional exclusion. Added .fab (position:fixed bottom-right, disabled treatment, icon-btn-derived visual language) and .create-sheet plus its destination/action row styling (mirroring .reply-sheet/.keys-pad chrome already in the file). No existing CSS rule was changed.

trace.verify_output: tsc --noEmit: clean (0 errors). vitest test/switcher.test.ts: Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 11 passed (11), Duration 562ms. Full suite (npm run test -- --run, all 4 files): Test Files 4 passed (4), Tests 43 passed (43). npm run bundle: vite build succeeded, 13 modules transformed, output 310.58 kB / gzip 78.82 kB.
